page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton processor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores built on the arm64 ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is delivered via a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves top-tier results in large-block read and write memory bandwidth, as well as in the Passmark memory score. Database operations also rank in the top tier, reflecting the high-throughput capabilities of the local NVMe storage and memory architecture. General compute metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, place the server in the strong tier, while stress-ng CPU performance and Redis SET operations remain in the average tier.

While the server excels in memory bandwidth and database operations, it exhibits performance limitations in specific workloads. Large-model LLM inference prompt processing and small-model text generation benchmarks fall into the poor tier, indicating that this architecture is not optimal for heavy machine learning inference tasks without dedicated GPU acceleration. Given the high local storage capacity, top-tier memory bandwidth, and strong database benchmark results, this instance is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput transactional applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 24-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of memory and a substantial 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in large-block memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong Geekbench and decompression scores. However, the instance exhibits poor performance in certain LLM inference tasks, reflecting its lack of dedicated GPU hardware. This resource profile makes the i8ge.6xlarge highly efficient for data-intensive workloads, such as high-throughput databases, transactional systems, and memory-heavy data processing, where local storage capacity and memory bandwidth are critical.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring a 24-core Graviton ARMv9 processor, 192 GB RAM, 15,000 GB local NVMe SSD, and top-tier memory and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 24 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized ARMv9 instance featuring massive local NVMe capacity and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 24-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor
Equipped with 15,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Features 192.0 GB of system memory with top-tier large-block bandwidth
Delivers 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for high-throughput databases and memory-intensive transactional workloads
